actionItems.what:
Interlock permanent fixture: All interlocks must use one-way screws. Retrofit machines may need screw heads ground off or interlock relocated out of reach. Need to determine if this requires a new work item or falls under existing retrofit scope (#72188).

UPDATE: HML is actively implementing captive screws with E4 retaining rings per #68074 (34 comments of discussion). Solution tested, Nick approved, HML proceeding. Applies to all EU machines, all brands.

Safety card encoder compatibility: Confirm safety card works with ALL encoder types used on torque motors (VCX table, SRT B/C, single-encoder feedback). Also determine if these encoders meet MTTF/SIL2 requirements â€” Fred has the spreadsheet for this. - attached to #58647

ANSWERED â€” Rotary axes require SIL2, NOT SIL3. Per ISO 16090-1:2022 Annex J: PLr d / Cat 3 / SIL 2 for safety stop on guard opening, prevention of unexpected startup, SLS at 15 m/min or 50 rpm. Yaskawa mechanical tables OK with safety card (direct encoder not in safety loop).

Confirm add-on rotary tables per division: Hurco-provided tables with Yaskawa drives should comply using safety card â€” NEEDS CONFIRMED. Each division must evaluate their own add-on tables.   Confirmed, universal rotary table uses sigma-5 which does work with the safety card.
Cytec heads: Identify what motors, drives, and encoders are used. Needed to determine safety card applicability and SIL2 compliance.

Rexroth lathe spindles: Confirm whether safety card is already present but unused, needs to be added, or if spindle simply cannot run with door open. Applies to lathe models with Rexroth-driven spindles. (need input from BrandonK or MingC)

Barfeeders: Must be wired into machine estop and cannot feed with door open. Confirm if current implementation already prevents this â€” if not, may need to wire into door circuit. Likely needs a work item. (electrical needs to confirm schematics are correct)

PILS for deadman / hold-to-run on v11 lathes: RJU needs PILS for jogging linear axes (and maybe Rexroth C axis) with doors open. Retrofit side already captured in #72188 description ("Additional PILZ for hold-to-run button second contact"). Production machines need this scoped too.
DCX door switches: Manual releases must be on inside of enclosure.

Milltronics / Delta: Inspire+ is good for deadman and 2-hand control to cabinet. Delta FSoE already tracked in #64593.

UPDATE: Delta A3 drives confirmed SIL3/FSoE capable with safety card (per Stewart & Ian in #64593, Oct 2025). Stewart requested safety card from Delta for testing. STILL OPEN: Does each Milltronics model need a PILS unit?
Procobots: Evaluate safety requirements. No details from 3/5 meeting â€” need to define scope.

Define ALL retrofit kits and production changes ASAP â€” especially long lead items. Door interlocks need cyclic monitoring which requires v11 PLC changes. Retrofit kits tracked in #72188 (non-SafePLC) and #62640 (SafePLC). Production changes need scoping. - repurposed #62640 from safe plc to production changes

DECISION NEEDED: Repurpose #62640 (Safety PLC - Create retrofit kits) for production changes? #72188 already covers non-SafePLC retrofits for Europe. Having both is confusing â€” clarify scope or merge.

UPDATE: Stewart confirmed in #62602 (3/16/2026) that SafePLC will NOT be used to retrofit EU machines. So: #72188 = retrofit kits (safety cards, PILZ, door mods, no SafePLC). #62640 = SafePLC production kits. Both are needed, different scope.

meetingNotes.notes:
Meeting 3/5/2026:  Ryan, Dave, Paul, Ian, Michael, Robert attending. 


â€¢	Door interlocks need cyclic monitoring to avoid high coded interlocks. *** v11 PLC changes needed.  
â€¢	All interlocks must be permanently fixtured, one way screws.***  Retrofit machines could have the screw heads ground off.  OR possible to move the interlock so it is not accessible.        
â€¢	A1000 and SD + current motor and encoder cannot use saftey card.  We must not allow the spindle to run wit hthe doors open. 
â€¢	Any torque motor (single encoder feedback, VCX table, SRT B and C, etc) will need the saftey cards.  Need to confirm if card will work with all encoder types.  Also need to determine these encoders meet the MTTF/SIL2 (Fred spreadsheet).  
â€¢	Do rotary axes need to meet SIL3? 
o	Mechanical tables using yaskawa motors should be ok with the saftey card.  Direct encoder is not used in saftey loop with the drive and card.  
o	Add on rotary tables must be evaluated per division.  We believe Hurco provided tables using yaskawa drives will comply if using the saftey card, NEEDS CONFIRMED.
o	Cytec heads, evaluate what motors/drives/encoders are used.
â€¢	Lathes:  Machines with A1000 driven spindles must not run with the door open. TM, TMX plain.  Live tool models cannot run the live tool with the doors open. 
â€¢	Lathes with rexroth driven spindles either the spindle cannot run with the door open OR the saftey may need to be added or is there but not currently used.  NEEDS CONFIRMED.
â€¢	Turrets cannot index with the doors open because A1000 cannot run with the door open.  On MAX5 the 2 hand control is not saftey rated because it is using CANBus.      
â€¢	v11 lathes need the added PILS for deadman on RJU for jogging linear axes (maybe rexroth driven C) with the doors open.
â€¢	Lathes with yaskawa drives (sigma 5/10) need saftey cards.  
â€¢	Barfeeders?  Must be wired into machine estop.  Cannot feed with the door open, do we currently do this?******  May need to be wired in with door circuit.   
â€¢	ATC not allowed with door open*******  ouch.
â€¢	Line filters must be used and tested for EMI worst case senarios per machine class (TMX plain, TMX10MYS, VMX42SRT, VC500, VCX600).  
o	If MYS passes we can assume Rexroth systems with line filters are ok for EMI (DCX, HBMX, etc).    
â€¢	DCX fix door switches, manual releases must be on inside of enclosure.  
â€¢	Milltronics?  Delta.  Inspire plus is good for deadman and 2 hand control to the cabinet.  PILS unit may need to be added for each.  
â€¢	Procobots?        
â€¢	Define all retrofit kits and production changes.  ASAP.  Especially long lead items. ******
â€¢	Takumi?  Where is CE technical file?  Located here:  https://hurcocompany.sharepoint.com/sites/Partner/Takumi/Certifications/Forms/AllItems.aspx
